topic: An artist's work is displayed without their permission
raw_output:
[story 1]

The gallery lights were too bright, sterile and unforgiving. I stood at the edge of the room, my breath catching in my throat as I took in the canvases. My latest series, the ones I’d poured months of my soul into, were hung on pristine white walls, bathed in an unwelcome spotlight. A wave of icy nausea washed over me. I moved closer, my footsteps silent on the polished floor, my gaze snagging on the vibrant blues and turbulent reds. Someone—a curator, perhaps, or some faceless agent—was standing near the centerpiece, a faint, proprietary smile playing on their lips. I clenched my jaw so tightly my teeth ached, wishing with every fiber of my being that I could simply evaporate into the expensive air. The silence in my head was deafening, punctuated only by the frantic drumming of my pulse against my ribs.

[story 2]

"This is... unexpected," the gallery owner murmured, swirling a glass of champagne. I felt a tremor start low in my stomach, a physical tightening that made my hands sweat beneath my silk sleeve. They were talking about the pieces, about their market value, completely bypassing the fact that these were *mine*. I watched as a group of patrons clustered around the work, nodding approvingly, their admiration feeling like a sharp, physical intrusion. I backed away slowly, my eyes darting from one painting to the next, cataloging every brushstroke as if searching for a flaw, a sign of trespass. A profound sense of violation settled over me, heavy and suffocating, making the air feel thin and hard to draw into my lungs. I needed to leave, needed to escape the suffocating scrutiny.

[story 3]

I found myself lingering by the entrance, watching the audience mill about the exhibited collection. Every time someone paused to study a piece, their attention felt like a physical prod against my skin. My knuckles were white where I gripped the strap of my small bag. The colors—my colors—seemed to mock me from the wall, radiating an energy I wasn't allowed to control or interpret. A sharp, involuntary shudder ran through my frame. I tried to focus on the architectural details of the ceiling, anything to anchor myself, but the sheer audacity of the display kept pulling my focus back to the intrusion. I took a deep, ragged breath, tasting dust and anxiety, and started walking toward the exit, every step weighted with a heavy, internal resistance.
